evals.prompt: I just changed how we handle JWT token expiry in the auth middleware. The old code just checked exp claim, the new code also checks a server-side revocation list in Redis. Can you review this before I commit?
evals.expected_output: Full commit review report including: Change Map identifying the auth middleware file, Intent Analysis classifying as security fix, Evidence section with at least 2 sources (JWT spec or OWASP session management + Redis docs or similar), Risk Matrix calling out the Redis dependency and rollback complexity, a suggested conventional commit message with type 'security' or 'fix', PR description, and a confidence score with detailed breakdown.
assertions: The report includes an Executive Summary
The report classifies the change as security-related (fix, security, or feat type)
The Evidence section contains at least 2 sources
The Risk Matrix includes a row for Security dimension
The Risk Matrix includes a row for Operations dimension (Redis dependency)
A Conventional Commit message is generated with type prefix
A Confidence Score is provided with a numeric value out of 100
The confidence score interpretation is stated (Ready / Needs review / etc.)

evals.prompt: review my staged changes and help me write a commit message
evals.expected_output: Runs git status and git diff --staged, builds a Change Map from the actual staged files, infers the intent, and generates a conventional commit message with Why/Impact/Evidence/Risk body. If no staged changes are found, asks the user to stage files first or checks unstaged diff.
assertions: The skill runs git status or git diff --staged to inspect the repository
A Change Map table is produced
An Intent Analysis section is included
A Conventional Commit message is generated
The commit message follows type(scope): summary format
A Confidence Score is included

evals.prompt: I upgraded React from 17 to 18 and also updated the router config to use createBrowserRouter instead of BrowserRouter. Lots of files changed. Can you do a commit forensics review?
evals.expected_output: Full review identifying this as a migration + refactor, grouping changes into dependency upgrade and API migration categories. Evidence section should reference React 18 migration guide and React Router v6 docs. Risk Matrix highlights potential concurrent mode side-effects and router API differences. Confidence score reflects the complexity of the migration.
assertions: The Change Map groups changes into logical categories (dependency upgrade, router migration, etc.)
The Intent Analysis classifies this as migration or refactor
The Evidence section references React 18 or React Router documentation
The Risk Matrix includes Correctness risks (potential rendering behavior changes)
A PR description is generated
A Confidence Score is provided
Follow-up Tasks section lists at least one item given the complexity of the migration
